---
title: "Molecules of interest for aromas"
---

[![Limonene is present in the citrus aromas in orange peels.](limoneno.png){width="40%"}](https://chemapps.stolaf.edu/jmol/jmol.php?model=CC1=CCC(CC1)C(=C)C)

<p style="text-align:right;">
[Wikipedia](https://pt.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limonene)
</p>

[![Vanillin is present in the aroma of vanilla.](vanilina.png){width="40%"}](https://chemapps.stolaf.edu/jmol/jmol.php?model=COC1=C(C=CC(=C1)C=O)O)

<p style="text-align:right;">
[Wikipedia](https://pt.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vanilina)
</p>

[![Geraniol is present in the aroma of roses.](geraniol.png){width="40%"}](https://chemapps.stolaf.edu/jmol/jmol.php?model=CC(=CCC/C(=C/CO)/C)C)

<p style="text-align:right;">
[Wikipedia](https://pt.wikipedia.org/wiki/Geraniol)
</p>

[![Eugenol is present in the aroma of cloves.](eugenol.png){width="40%"}](https://chemapps.stolaf.edu/jmol/jmol.php?model=COC1=C(C=CC(=C1)CC=C)O)

<p style="text-align:right;">
[Wikipedia](https://pt.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eugenol)
</p>
